title: Aurelia Jeffery Traveling the Cruce Andino Route Through Bariloche Río Negro Argentina Ferry Crossing
description: Cruce Andino, Río Negro: Aurelia Jeffery rests peacefully in her seat, finding comfort and nourishment during the transit across the pristine Andean lakes. The soft, diffuse light filtering through the ferry windows illuminates the gentle contours of her expression, creating an atmosphere of quiet reflection amidst the grand scale of the Patagonian wilderness. This candid capture highlights the rhythm of family exploration, where the simple necessities of travel balance against the backdrop of one of the world's most dramatic border crossings. With a shallow depth of field that isolates the toddler from the structured textures of the cabin seating, the composition emphasizes the intimacy of a small moment within an expansive geographic journey. The seamless integration of snack time with the motion of the vessel underscores the endurance required for such an ambitious international passage. Such scenes are essential to understanding the reality of slow, overland travel, which often involves navigating the needs of the youngest passengers through shifting landscapes. By framing this moment, the photographers draw attention to the human element that anchors even the most monumental expeditions in the southern Andes. The transition from Argentina to Chile requires patience and logistics, yet it yields a profound connection to the surrounding mountain environment. Ultimately, this documentation serves to personalize the rugged transit route, showing how families weave their own narrative into the fabric of the region's long-standing tourism history. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
